info.name: Approach3-DepegStress-MultiContract-100K-30d-iter4
info.description:
APPROACH 3 (iter4): DEDICATED DEPEG STRESS SIMULATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

WHY A SEPARATE SIMULATION FROM MINT-HALT-RESTORE:
  MintHaltRestore tests: liquidity breach → binary halt → T-bill maturity → restore
  DepegStress tests:     progressive peg deviation → redemption cascade → reserve
                         adequacy → secondary market impact → recovery/failure

  These are DIFFERENT risk scenarios:
  - MintHalt can trigger WITHOUT depeg (pure liquidity mismatch)
  - Depeg can worsen WITHOUT halt (reserves adequate but peg breaks)
  - In crisis, both interact — but each needs isolated testing first

SCENARIO: PROGRESSIVE DEPEG (modeled on USDC/SVB March 2023 pattern)
  Phase 1: BASELINE — peg perfect, all reserves intact
  Phase 2: MILD DEPEG (0.5%) — early warning triggers, mild redemptions
  Phase 3: MODERATE DEPEG (1.5%) — 609-event model: 5.7%/day redemptions
  Phase 4: SEVERE DEPEG (3.0%) — 14.2%/day redemptions, backing at risk
  Phase 5: PEAK CRISIS (5.0%) — 28.5%/day redemptions, reserve adequacy test
  Phase 6: RECOVERY or FAILURE — peg restoring vs death spiral

BEHAVIORAL MODELS ACTIVE:
  All 10 from IntegratedBehavioral-iter4, PLUS:
  - DepegSeverityModel — progressive peg loss tracking
  - SecondaryMarketModel — DEX/CEX price impact
  - RedemptionCascadeModel — cumulative redemption tracking
  - RecoveryProbabilityModel — 609-event survival prediction

CALIBRATION (from 609-event de-peg database):
  0.5% depeg → 2.3%/day redemptions → 94% survival if liq>35%
  1.0% depeg → 5.7%/day → 67% survival
  3.0% depeg → 14.2%/day → 34% survival
  5.0% depeg → 28.5%/day → 12% survival
  10%+ depeg → 52%/day → <5% survival (algorithmic death spiral)

PORTFOLIO: Same $100K 7-contract portfolio as IntegratedBehavioral

PREREQUISITES:
  - actus-server on localhost:8083
  - risksrv3 on localhost:8082 (uses same models from IntegratedBehavioral setup)
  - Run IntegratedBehavioral Phase 1-2 first (loads risk factors)
  - Or run this standalone (Phase 1 loads its own reference indexes)

KEY DIFFERENCE FROM USDC/SVB:
  USDC depeg was EXOGENOUS (SVB bank failure) — not reserve quality issue
  This simulation models ENDOGENOUS depeg — where reserve stress CAUSES the depeg
  Both patterns are tested: exogenous shock + endogenous cascade

exec: console.log('   Modeled on USDC/SVB pattern: sharp rise, gradual recovery');

item.name: 1.2 Add SC_RATE_DEPEG — Rate curve during depeg
